descript.abstract: This dataset displays miles of habitat improved upstream from an Aquatic Organism Passage (AOP) structure that was improved by an on the ground activity. Data includes the completed fiscal year and lists species that benefit from the habitat improvement. The miles of habitat improved are displayed as a line or multi-line. Data are from USFS Natural Resource Manager Watershed Improvement Tracking (WIT) database.
descript.purpose: Provide spatial and tabular information about the miles of habitat improved upstream from an activity on an Aquatic Organism Passage (AOP) structure.

timeperd.current: Temporal Extent of data: April 2012-current. Data are current as of publication date.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse
cntorgp.cntper: National Applications Liaison Office | US Forest Service (usda.gov)

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: This data is based on field gathered observations. No specific quality check or measures are available.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication.

procstep.procdesc: The source of this dataset is the Natural Resource Manager (NRM) database of the US Forest Service. The aquatic organism passage data are pulled from the oracle view, fs_nris_wit.nrwi_habitat_miles_vw. This data is published using Feature Manipulation Engine Software. The spatial features were extracted from the transactional feature class fs_nris_wit.nris_wit_site_pt and published to the target feature class at EDW.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: USFS Region

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value of the USFS region. See https://www.fs.usda.gov/about-agency/contact-us/regional-offices for more info

attr.attrdef: Forest Unit ID responsible for the activity.

attrdomv.udom: 4 digit numerical code. List of codes and their spatial extents can be viewed on the USFS AGOL site here: https://usfs.maps.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=09e4c1162a4d4af3a84163cbc76108c4

attr.attrdef: Forest Unit Name responsible for the activity.

attrdomv.udom: Text values. List of USFS Forest Unit names and their spatial extents can be viewed on the USFS AGOL site here: https://usfs.maps.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=09e4c1162a4d4af3a84163cbc76108c4

attr.attrdef: User provided unique ID for the activity.
User provided descriptive name for the activity.
Name of class of activity taking place
An abbreviated name for the type of activity taking place at the site.
User provided name for the habitat area made accessible by improving the stream crossing. Also known as the Affected Zone Name.
Miles of stream habitat made accessible to aquatic species by improving the stream crossing.
The fiscal year the activity was completed on the ground. Fiscal year begins October 1 and ends September 30.
Date the activity was completed on the ground.
Indicates whether ESA species benefitted from the activity

List of Endangered Species Act (ESA) listed species who that may have benefitted from the activity. This list may include ESA listed species, species populations, Distinct Population Segments (DPSs), and/or Evolutionarily Significant Units (ESUs).

attrdomv.udom: Semicolon delimited list of ESA listed species, species populations, DPSs, and/or ESUs

attr.attrdef: List of non-ESA listed species benefitted from the activity.

attrdomv.udom: Semicolon delineated list of text value

attr.attrdef: Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the spatial feature for the affected zone.
Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the activity.
Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the plan phase.
Feature geometry.
System generated shape length

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse
cntorgp.cntper: National Applications Liaison Office | US Forest Service (usda.gov)

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
